MAKE MUSIC, YOUR WAY

Play Play instruments live, sketch melodies, and control your session with MIDI or your keyboard. Record Record vocals, instruments, samples, and ideas straight into your project. Sound Design Shape synths, samples, effects, and signal chains until the sound feels yours. Mix Balance levels, route channels, add sends, and finish your track with control.

BUILD WHAT YOU WANT

Build your own

Turn your app, plugin, or music tool into something people can play together.

Let's Build

We invite musicians and developers to build together from idea to production-ready. Music creation apps, DAW plugins, games and remix tools.

Let's build together

Starting May 28

Your space to start and connect

MEET THE COMMUNITY

I took my first steps into music production with Audiotool, and it has simultaneously been a simple and accessible tool that anyone can learn to use, while also being an advanced software that I'm still growing in today.

Even with the crappiest setup, I have the tools available to make a fully professional track any time I have wifi. That's crazy!

The best thing about AT for me, is that users are not only given the tools to create music, but also an online space to share it with like-minded people who can learn from and inspire each other.

I really love its "complex simplicity". In my opinion, the learning curve for Audiotool is much more shallow than most other DAWs, which will help you get the basics down immediately.

The community side of the website is really what makes Audiotool so unique. On AT, everyone uses the same tools and DAW, so it's really easy to get feedback or tips on the devices.

I think the multitude of ways you can collaborate and work off of each other is amazing. If anyone starting out needs a helping hand there are thousands of hands out there waiting for you.

That feeling of being able to control everything on one screen and wire everything together is the closest thing to being in a real life studio I can imagine on a DAW.

One of my favorite things about audiotool is that it's so accessible. One of the reasons I haven't made the switch to any other standalone software suites is the fact that it's always right there at the push of a button.

I've been amazed hundreds of times by what can be created here, and felt so proud when artists I love were faving and commenting my tracks.

I don't know why but Audiotool has that "at the comfort of your home" feeling. It is so friendly and everything feels so familiar. Only thing I will suggest is health to the AT team that codes this wonderful place. Everything else will come and go. Being healthy is being wealthy.

I really love how easy it is to collaborate with someone. Two of you can be online at the same time, or you can go back and forth editing the same draft at different times.

It has to be that it's FRIGGIN FREE! Back when I had no money I was searching and searching for free softwares. I don't know where I'd be if it wasn't for Audiotool. I'm very grateful for this site.

Audiotool has raised up some amazing artists that if Audiotool wasn't as accessible as it is, might not have been able to make beautiful compositions and killer jams.

The most I like about this website is that it's free and can be used on literally anything.

Audiotool is so amazing that I'm just so grateful for the fact that it exists. Everything about it from the devices and layout, to the community and traditions are such a blessing.

I like the community the most on Audiotool: I like how friendly and welcoming people have become

How easy it is to just login, boot up from any computer, and get going. That portability and free access is really amazing

I like being able to express myself artistically for "the best price". But I also like seeing other people not only do the same but go onto do that professionally.

I love how it's a community integrated into a DAW, and how people can socialize and interact with an interface on a personal level. I've met some truly amazing artists and some long time friends due to this and I will forever thank audiotool for that.

I love the DIY and social nature of this place. We're all just a bunch of hobbyists on a niche German website exploring and enjoying the art of music creation together.

I love that the website offers the opportunity for users to give constructive criticism on my music. The community on Audiotool has made a huge positive impact on my development as an artist and a person.

I've had the pleasure to meet many fantastic artists that have reached out and gave feedback throughout the years which reshaped how I approach and create music.

What I like the most about it is that it's free, and that it has in-real-time collaborations.

This app is unique in the fact that there's a whole community of users with different styles, backgrounds and experiences built directly into the infrastructure of the site.

Seeing the work that other artists are doing right here on Audiotool has opened my mind to sounds, approaches, and techniques that I never would have imagined.

My favorite part of Audiotool is the sheer amount of variety of things you can do in the studio. You can literally make any sound you want, for free.

The thing which is really unique about Audiotool is that it allows you to compose live with other people, on the same project. It's a great value and we should develop that to improve our community.

You can do so much on Audiotool, and I believe you can take it to the next level if you put your best efforts into making something you enjoy and love.

The essence of Audiotool is as much of a community as it is a tool for others. It's the constant encouragement of ourselves from others in the community that makes us strive to get better.

I'm so grateful and really admire the work you are doing, guys. That's why I want everybody to support Audiotool on Patreon. The collab thing is also a great idea: you can chat with your colleague making music and discuss live what to do to your track!

I love the dev team and everyone involved with the building, maintaining, and growth of this site. You make this all possible, and bring so much light into people's lives with this small music app.

Audiotool has undoubtedly changed my life. With it I discovered my love of music, a passion that will certainly define me for as long as I live.

I love this way of making music by using the internet. The interface is so friendly to me. I love the way of patching cables and it reminds me of VCV Rack.

I think Audiotool is absolutely fantastic for a free DAW. What I love most is the endless possibilities of interconnecting devices. There really are no limits.

The thing I like about Audiotool the most, is the fact that it's free and allows live-time collaboration with other producers. Not many DAWs achieve this, the fact that you can pack this much power on something that is on the web alone still continues to wow me.

I love the browser based aspect and the community of course. That's what makes AT special. I'm very grateful for the friends I've made here and hope to continue to use this site as long as possible.

One thing that I find very unique about Audiotool is the ability to open up other people's tracks and see how they're made. I've learnt so much from doing this.

I took my first steps into music production with Audiotool, and it has simultaneously been a simple and accessible tool that anyone can learn to use, while also being an advanced software that I'm still growing in today.

1 / 37

Templates

Start faster with ready-made projects, patterns, and setups you can open and make your own.

Learn

Pick up the tools as you go with guides, examples, and practical ways to build tracks.

Connect

Find producers, follow artists, share ideas, and build with people who make music.

Apps

Open community-made tools that plug into projects and expand what you can create.

HEAR WHAT'S POSSIBLE

Join the community, and find the people who push your tracks further

Synth, drum machines, and effects

Build with award-winning synths, drum machines, samples, and effects made for everything from retro tones to modern sound design.

Synthesize your sound

Four-operator phase-modulation synthesizer with flexible modulation matrix and dynamic control. Perfect for digital pianos, bells, basses, and experimental textures.

Show all

Program drum patterns

Powerful drum machine with extensive sound design capabilities. Create everything from classic drum sounds to experimental percussion.

Show all

Apply effect chains

Create your own sound with built-in tools or your VST3 plugins

Play live and control your session with MIDI

Sketch ideas quickly

Play a part, catch the idea, and turn it into notes before the moment disappears.

MIDI learn

Map knobs, keys, and controls so your hardware feels connected to the session.

Capture notes and automation

Record notes, gestures, and parameter moves while you play.

Compose and arrange

Edit clips, build patterns, and move from first loop to full arrangement.

Compose chords, melody, and bass together

Write the musical parts side by side, then refine notes, timing, velocity, and automation in the piano roll.

Start with your hands on the keys and build the session around what feels right

Record audio right into your session

Build from ready-to-use
sample library

Browse sounds, drop them into your project, and start building without hunting for files.

Stay in time with latency
compensation

Record takes that stay aligned, even when your setup adds delay.

Cut and stretch

Trim, move, loop, and stretch audio until it fits the track.

Shift pitch with control

Tune samples and recordings without losing the shape of the idea.

Warp samples and recordings

Stretch timing, shift pitch, and reshape recordings so vocals, loops, and samples sit in the groove.

Bring vocals, instruments, loops, and found sounds straight into the session

Go deep with sound synthesis

Create with built-in instruments and effects

Start with synths, drum machines, effects, and utilities already inside the studio.

Bring in your VST3 plugins

Use your own instruments and effects alongside Audiotool’s native devices.

Shape your sound

Bend tone, movement, space, and texture until the patch has its own character.

Automate any parameter

Draw motion into filters, levels, effects, synth controls, and plugin parameters.

Design unique sounds with modular processing

Connect instruments, effects, plugins, and utilities into custom signal chains that behave like your own studio rack.

Patch instruments, effects, and plugins into sounds that feel built, not borrowed

Mix your session until it feels alive

Find the balance

Set levels, pan tracks, and make every part sit where it belongs.

Group and master inserts

Process groups and the master with insert chains that keep the mix under control.

Filter, Compressor, EQ

Clean up tone, control dynamics, and carve space for each sound.

Create custom FX sends

Send multiple tracks into shared reverb, delay, distortion, or your own effect chains.

Mix width depth and precision

Balance channels, shape groups, add sends, and keep the master chain in view while the whole session comes together.

Balance the session, shape the space, and make every part feel like one track

Trusted by leaders and innovators

From Spitfire Audio to Fraunhofer IDMT, we build Audiotool with partners who help shape the future of music.

  • We're thrilled to have Spitfire as the first plugin partner integrated into Audiotool. Audiotool is the most professional cloud-based DAW we've seen, pairing a powerful workflow with a vibrant, collaborative creator community.

    Paul Gathercole

    CTO at Spitfire Audio (acquired by Splice)

  • Audiotool will enable creators to work directly with Fraunhofer's AI tools within real music production workflows. Production actions recorded in a structured, time-based format let Fraunhofer AI services improve workflows in ways traditional DAWs cannot.

    Hanna Lukashevich

    R&D Management Leader, Fraunhofer Institute for Digital Media Technology IDMT

  • BandM8 was created to give music creators a smart, nimble creative partner. Launching first with Audiotool feels right because they've built a DAW around easy access, community, exploration, and learning by doing.

    Robert Pfeifer

    CEO and Co-Founder, BandM8
